articles.title: Ops Kesan 6.0 begins June 1 to curb profiteering amid global supply crisis
articles.description: PUTRAJAYA: The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry will conduct Ops Kesan 6.0 from June 1 to Aug 31 to curb profiteering amid the increase in world crude oil prices resulting from the global supply crisis.

articles.title: U.S. sanctions on Russia’s oil industry: Global energy markets in turmoil as Washington tightens the screws
articles.description: The Trump administration imposed sweeping sanctions on Russian oil majors Rosneft and Lukoil on October 22, 2025, targeting Moscow’s revenue for the Ukraine war. Oil prices surged dramatically, with Brent crude exceeding $140 per barrel and U.S. West Texas Intermediate surpassing $130 per barrel. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent announced a 30-day sanctions waiver extension on […]

articles.title: Pakistan reduces petrol, diesel prices by ₹22 per litre, third such move in recent weeks
articles.description: Pakistan cuts petrol and HSD prices by ₹22 per litre, offers Eid relief to consumers despite global energy tensions and ongoing US Iran ceasefire talks

articles.title: Bessent’s net worth comes under fire after ‘less than $200 in extra gas cost’ remark
articles.description: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent’s reassurance that the war with Iran will end soon comes amidst rising inflation and an increase in gasoline prices. However, his remarks sparked outrage as his net worth of nearly a billion dollars and privileged position were called out, with politicians criticizing him for being out of touch. | financialexpress.com

articles.title: US stock markets this week: S&P 500 posts 9th straight weekly gain as tech stocks power markets to new records
articles.description: US stock markets closed at record levels amid easing geopolitical tensions and lower oil prices. The S&P 500 marked its fourth consecutive record high, while technology stocks, driven by AI growth, led the rally despite concerns over ongoing Middle East tensions.

articles.title: Oil prices tumble 11% for biggest weekly drop in 7 weeks. Where is liquid gold headed from here?
articles.description: Global markets rallied as oil prices hit seven-week lows. Hopes for a peace deal between the US, Israel, and Iran eased inflation fears. Brent crude and WTI saw sharp declines. Diplomatic efforts continue, but differing descriptions of a potential deal persist. Geopolitical tensions remain a factor. Analysts suggest restoring normal shipping could take months.

articles.title: The ultimate guide to cheaper summer holidays
articles.description: From low air fares to cheap petrol, great-value supermarket shopping and hotels vs self-catering – how to save in the peak weeks

articles.title: Australia extends measure that releases petrol and diesel from domestic reserves
articles.description: CANBERRA: Australia on Saturday (May 30) extended a measure that releases petrol and diesel from domestic reserves, Energy Minister Chris Bowen ​said, as the country continues to grapple with the impact of the Iran war on energy supplies.
